diagrams.title: Unfolded RNN through Time
diagrams.description: Shows a 3-time-step unfolded RNN with shared weights
elements: x_0, x_1, x_2: Input at each time step
h_0, h_1, h_2: Hidden states
y_0, y_1, y_2: Outputs
W_xh, W_hh, W_hy: Shared weights (same across all steps)
Arrows showing forward flow (solid) and backward gradient flow (dashed)
diagrams.purpose: Visualize how recurrent connections become feedforward connections after unfolding

diagrams.title: BPTT Forward and Backward Pass
diagrams.description: Complete BPTT workflow showing forward pass through sequence then backward gradient computation
elements: Forward pass: compute h_t and y_t for t=0 to τ
Store all hidden states
Compute loss at each time step
Backward pass: gradients flow from loss back to h_τ, then through each time step
Show gradient accumulation for shared weights
diagrams.purpose: Understand the complete BPTT algorithm workflow and data flow

diagrams.title: Gradient Vanishing in BPTT
diagrams.description: Illustration showing gradient magnitude decay through time steps
elements: Time step 0 at far left, time step τ at right
Gradient bars getting progressively smaller as we go backward
Multiplication symbols showing repeated derivative multiplication
Comparison: short-term dependency (strong gradient) vs long-term dependency (weak gradient)
diagrams.purpose: Visualize why distant time step influences are hard to learn in standard RNNs

diagrams.title: RNN Cell Computation
diagrams.description: Internal computation within a single RNN time step
elements: Input x_t and previous hidden state h_{t-1}
Weight matrices W_xh and W_hh
Addition operation
tanh activation function
Output hidden state h_t
Equation: h_t = tanh(W_xh·x_t + W_hh·h_{t-1} + b_h)
diagrams.purpose: Understand the fundamental computation unit in RNN that BPTT operates on
